name: Ability Check
description: An ability check tests a creature's innate talent and training in an effort to overcome a challenge. The DM calls for an ability check when a creature attempts an action (other than an attack) that has a chance of failure. When the outcome is uncertain, the dice determine the results.
effects: Roll 1d20 and add the relevant ability modifier
If you have proficiency in the relevant skill, add your Proficiency Bonus
The DM sets a DC (Difficulty Class); meet or exceed the DC to succeed
Typical DCs: Very Easy (5), Easy (10), Medium (15), Hard (20), Very Hard (25), Nearly Impossible (30)

name: Skill Checks
description: Each ability covers a broad range of capabilities, including skills that a character or monster can be proficient in. A skill represents a specific aspect of an ability score, and an individual's proficiency in a skill demonstrates a focus on that aspect.
effects: Strength: Athletics
Dexterity: Acrobatics, Sleight of Hand, Stealth
Intelligence: Arcana, History, Investigation, Nature, Religion
Wisdom: Animal Handling, Insight, Medicine, Perception, Survival
Charisma: Deception, Intimidation, Performance, Persuasion

name: Proficiency Bonus
description: Your Proficiency Bonus is added to ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws that use a skill, tool, weapon, or saving throw in which you have proficiency. The bonus is determined by your character level.
effects: Levels 1-4: +2
Levels 5-8: +3
Levels 9-12: +4
Levels 13-16: +5
Levels 17-20: +6
You never add your Proficiency Bonus more than once to a single roll

name: Advantage and Disadvantage
description: Advantage and Disadvantage reflect the extraordinary circumstances surrounding a d20 roll. When you have Advantage, you roll two d20s and use the higher result. When you have Disadvantage, you roll two d20s and use the lower result.
effects: Advantage: Roll 2d20, use the higher roll
Disadvantage: Roll 2d20, use the lower roll
If you have both Advantage and Disadvantage, they cancel each other out regardless of how many sources of each you have
You never roll more than two d20s for Advantage or Disadvantage
If multiple situations apply that grant Advantage, you still only roll two d20s

name: Passive Checks
description: A passive check is a special kind of ability check that doesn't involve any die rolls. It represents the average result for a task done repeatedly or can be used when the DM wants to secretly determine whether the characters succeed at something without rolling dice.
effects: Passive check total = 10 + all modifiers that normally apply to the check
If the creature has Advantage on the check, add 5 to the passive score
If the creature has Disadvantage on the check, subtract 5 from the passive score
Commonly used for Passive Perception to detect hidden creatures or traps

name: Contests
description: Sometimes one character's or monster's efforts are directly opposed to another's. This can occur when both of them are trying to do the same thing and only one can succeed, or when one is trying to prevent the other from accomplishing a goal.
effects: Each participant makes an ability check appropriate to the effort
Apply all relevant bonuses and penalties
The higher check result wins the contest
On a tie, the situation remains the same as before the contest

name: Tool Proficiency
description: Having proficiency with a tool allows you to add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check you make using that tool. Tool use is not tied to a single ability, since proficiency with a tool represents broader knowledge of its use.
effects: Artisan's Tools cover various crafting proficiencies
Gaming Sets include dice sets and card games
Musical Instruments include various instruments you can play
Thieves' Tools are used to disarm traps and open locks
If you have proficiency in both a skill and a tool relevant to a check, you still only add your Proficiency Bonus once

name: Saving Throws
description: A saving throw represents an attempt to resist a spell, a trap, a poison, a disease, or a similar threat. You don't normally decide to make a saving throw; you are forced to make one because your character or monster is at risk of harm.
effects: Roll 1d20 and add the appropriate ability modifier
Add your Proficiency Bonus if you are proficient in that saving throw
The DC is determined by the effect that causes the save
A success usually means the creature suffers no harm, or reduced harm, from the effect
Each class gives proficiency in at least two saving throws

name: Group Checks
description: When a number of individuals are trying to accomplish something as a group, the DM might ask for a group ability check. In such a situation, the characters who are skilled at a particular task help cover those who aren't.
effects: Everyone in the group makes the ability check
If at least half the group succeeds, the whole group succeeds
Otherwise, the group fails
Commonly used for group Stealth checks when sneaking together

name: Heroic Inspiration
description: Heroic Inspiration is a special reward the DM can give to a player character. It represents the character's extraordinary resolve or luck in the moment.
effects: The DM awards Heroic Inspiration to reward compelling roleplaying, clever thinking, or simply for fun
You can spend Heroic Inspiration to give yourself Advantage on a d20 roll
You must decide to spend it before you roll
You either have Heroic Inspiration or you don't; you can't stockpile multiple uses
If you already have Heroic Inspiration and gain it again, you can give it to another player character
